subject: ToolGraph — resource and provider intelligence graph (third memory graph)
title: ToolGraph — Spoke and Hub Provider/Account/Cost Intelligence
what: Defines ToolGraph as the third intelligence graph alongside PathGraph (history/drift) and TasteGraph (identity/preferences). ToolGraph maps every tool, API, model, and service a spoke or the hub accesses: provider, account_id (supports multiple accounts per provider), available models, monthly cost, current standing, and access method. At the hub level, fleet synthesis aggregates across all spokes and surfaces optimization opportunities (cost arbitrage, underused capacity, lower-cost provider alternatives). ToolGraph also serves as the gate for VectorLibrary adoption: a spoke's access_matrix must contain all requires_tools declared by a vector before adoption is permitted.
why: Agents currently have no structured awareness of what tools they work with, which accounts fund them, or what they cost. This blindness causes: (1) suboptimal model routing — Navigator picks without knowing what's accessible; (2) invisible cost accumulation — no fleet-wide view of monthly spend; (3) unsafe vector adoption — spokes adopt patterns requiring tools they don't have. ToolGraph closes all three gaps. It also enables the hub to detect fleet-wide optimization opportunities, e.g. 30 spokes paying for Haiku when a local Ollama equivalent is free.
audience: Navigator — reads spoke ToolGraph to route to the best available model within the spoke's access matrix
Sentinel — reads ToolGraph to know which provider account each tool call routes through; alerts on rate limit or cost spike
AP — reads ToolGraph to budget-aware plan (goal value must exceed tool cost)
Hub Gardener — aggregates spoke ToolGraphs into fleet synthesis nightly
VectorLibrary adoption gate — checks access_matrix before recording adoption
use_cases: Model routing: Navigator reads WAI-Spoke/ToolGraph.json access_matrix to pick the highest-quality model within this spoke's subscriptions
Cost optimization: hub ToolGraph-fleet.json shows 30 spokes using anthropic-haiku at $X/month; generates recommendation to evaluate Ollama local alternative with adoption impact analysis
Vector adoption gate: spoke wants to adopt vector requiring [supabase-rest, haiku]; ToolGraph access_matrix confirms both present — adoption proceeds
Multi-account awareness: spoke has two Anthropic accounts (personal at $50/mo, client at $200/mo); ToolGraph tracks both with separate monthly_cost and current_standing
Sentinel monitoring: Sentinel reads ToolGraph to know which account a tool call routes through; fires alert when account hits rate limit or cost spike
patterns: Spoke-local: WAI-Spoke/ToolGraph.json; agent-maintained — updated when new tools are discovered or account details change
Hub fleet synthesis: hub/WAI-Hub/ToolGraph-fleet.json aggregated from all spoke ToolGraph.json files on nightly run
access_matrix: flat list of {tool_id, provider, account_id} tuples — the authoritative gate for VectorLibrary adoption checks
optimization_opportunities[]: hub-level actionable list with estimated_savings_usd and adoption_impact narrative
Account-aware tracking: tool_id alone is not sufficient — (tool_id, account_id) is the unique spoke access record when multiple accounts exist

components.tool_id: string — canonical slug e.g. anthropic-haiku, supabase-rest, github-api, ollama-local
components.category: model | database | api | runtime | storage | identity
components.provider: string — e.g. anthropic, supabase, github, local, openai
components.account_id: string — which account/org is in use; required when provider has multiple accounts
components.account_label: string — human label e.g. personal, work, client-x
models: string — for model providers: list of accessible model_ids
components.monthly_cost_usd: float | null — last billing cycle spend; null if unknown
components.last_verified: ISO-8601 | null — when standing was last confirmed

hub_toolgraph_fleet.spoke_count: int — number of spokes included in this synthesis

<provider_id>.spoke_count: int — how many spokes use this provider
accounts: account_id strings — unique accounts across fleet

optimization_opportunities.adoption_impact: string — what changes when spokes adopt this recommendation
optimization_opportunities.recommendation: string — concrete action e.g. 'Run Haiku workloads via ollama-local instead; free after setup'

constraints: account_id is required when the same provider appears more than once in components[] — never collapse two accounts to one tool_id entry
monthly_cost_usd is best-effort and may be null; agents must never fabricate cost figures
Hub ToolGraph-fleet.json is read-only from spoke perspective — spokes never write directly to the hub file
access_matrix is the authoritative gate: if a tool_id is not listed there, it is not available in this spoke regardless of what components[] says
Fleet synthesis runs on hub nightly only; spokes must not trigger hub synthesis directly
ToolGraph.json is an agent-maintained file: agents update it when discovering new tools or observing account changes, not just on spec install
tests: Schema validation: ToolGraph.json with duplicate provider but missing account_id on one entry fails validation
Access gate: spoke access_matrix missing supabase-rest; attempt vector adoption with requires_tools=[supabase-rest] — blocked with clear reason
Access gate pass: all requires_tools present in access_matrix — adoption proceeds
Fleet synthesis: two spoke ToolGraphs submitted; hub synthesizes provider_summary with correct spoke_count and summed monthly_cost_usd
Optimization detection: 3 spokes use anthropic-haiku; hub generates an optimization_opportunity entry of type cost_arbitrage or local_alternative
current_standing: a component with current_standing=degraded appears in Sentinel watch list on next startup

open_questions: ToolGraph.json maintenance: agent-maintained on discovery vs. automated scan from MCP tool list on session start — which is primary?
Should access_matrix be auto-populated from MCP tool introspection at session start, or manually curated?
Fleet synthesis frequency: nightly only, or on-demand via hub query endpoint?

perceive: Read this spec fully. Check if WAI-Spoke/ToolGraph.json already exists. Read WAI-Spoke/advisors/navigator/recommendations-current.json for current model routing to understand what tool access is assumed. Read spec-vector-library-v1 for adoption gate integration points.
execute: 1. Create WAI-Spoke/ToolGraph.json seeding it with all tools currently known in use (Anthropic models, Supabase REST, GitHub API, GitNexus MCP). 2. Add access_matrix population step to session startup. 3. Add ToolGraph adoption gate check to vector adoption workflow (spec-vector-library-v1 integration). 4. Add ToolGraph.json update checkpoint to session closeout checklist. 5. Create hub/WAI-Hub/ToolGraph-fleet.json scaffold with empty optimization_opportunities[]. 6. Deliver impl-toolgraph-framework-seed-v1 lug to own incoming if not already present.
verify: 1. WAI-Spoke/ToolGraph.json parses against schema with no missing required fields. 2. access_matrix contains at least: anthropic-haiku, anthropic-sonnet, supabase-rest. 3. Attempt vector adoption with a tool missing from access_matrix — blocked with clear error. 4. Hub ToolGraph-fleet.json scaffold exists and is valid JSON. 5. Sentinel startup reads ToolGraph and flags any degraded components.
